title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 23, 2026: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh Heavily on Global Markets

items.headline: Global Markets Plunge Amid Escalating Geopolitical Conflict and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: Equity markets worldwide experienced significant downturns on Monday, March 23, 2026, as geopolitical tensions, particularly the escalating conflict in the Middle East, intensified investor anxiety. Surging energy prices and inflation concerns are driving market volatility, with US stock futures indicating a lower open.

items.statContext: Down 0.31% on March 23, 2026, from previous session.
US stock futures edged lower on Monday, March 23. The index closed down 0.96% on Friday, March 20, amidst escalating Middle East conflict and surging energy prices.
US stock futures edged lower on Monday, March 23. The index closed down 2.01% on Friday, March 20, hitting six-month lows.

items.summary: Visa recorded a gain among Dow components on Friday, March 20.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues Amidst Geopolitical Volatility
items.summary: A defined market rotation is underway, with the energy sector continuing to lead, gaining approximately 2.99% for the week ending March 20. Utilities and basic materials were among the worst-performing sectors, down 5.0% and 4.82% respectively for the same period. Investors are reportedly shifting away from software towards defense and energy stocks.

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist as Fed Holds Rates; PMI Data Expected
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates between 3.50% and 3.75% earlier this week, with inflation concerns still prominent following a 0.7% month-over-month rise in US producer prices in February. Investors are awaiting flash US PMI data due Tuesday for further economic insights. No noteworthy economic indicators were released today, March 23.

items.headline: WuXi AppTec Scheduled to Report Earnings Today
items.summary: WuXi AppTec ($WUXI) is scheduled to report key corporate earnings today, Monday, March 23, 2026.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Escalation Fuels Oil Price Surge and Global Economic Threat
items.summary: The ongoing war in the Middle East has entered its fourth week, leading to a significant surge in crude oil prices, with WTI crude hovering near $100 per barrel. The head of the International Energy Agency warned today that the global economy faces a 'major, major threat' from the escalating conflict, which has already caused Asian markets to plunge.
